2606.29999.title: AlgoSkill: Learning to Design Algorithms by Scheduling Human-Like Skills
2606.29999.paper_summary: Designing an algorithm from a natural-language problem statement requires identifying the problem structure, reading constraints, choosing a suitable paradigm, checking correctness, and refining complexity. Existing large language model (LLM) methods often rely on direct generation or generic self-refinement, leaving these steps implicit. We propose AlgoSkill, which models algorithm design as sequential decision-making over a typed library of algorithmic skills, including abstraction, constraint analysis, state design, data-structure selection, proof checking, counterexample construction, and complexity refinement. A learned scheduler proposes skills from the current design state, while a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) controller explores skill sequences using verification feedback from compilation, testing, stress testing, and complexity analysis. Experiments on competitive programming and combinatorial optimization benchmarks show that AlgoSkill improves over direct LLM generation, chain-of-thought prompting, self-refinement, and MCTS without typed skills. Ablations show that typed skills, verification-based repair, and search-based scheduling each contribute to performance. These results support treating automatic algorithm design as verification-guided skill scheduling rather than one-shot code generation.
2606.29999.paper_summary_zh: 設計一個基於自然語言問題陳述的算法需要識別問題結構、閱讀約束、選擇合適的範式、檢查正確性以及精煉複雜度。現有的大型語言模型（LLM）方法通常依賴於直接生成或通用自我精煉，將這些步驟隱含在內。我們提出了AlgoSkill，它將算法設計建模為在一個類型化的算法技能庫上進行的序列決策，包括抽象、約束分析、狀態設計、數據結構選擇、證明檢查、反例構造和複雜度精煉。一個學習的調度器根據當前設計狀態提出技能，而一個蒙特卡羅樹搜索（MCTS）控制器則利用來自編譯、測試、壓力測試和複雜度分析的驗證反饋來探索技能序列。在競爭性編程和組合優化基準上的實驗顯示，AlgoSkill在直接LLM生成、思路鏈提示、自我精煉和沒有類型技能的MCTS上都有所改進。消融實驗顯示，類型技能、基於驗證的修復和基於搜索的調度各自對性能有所貢獻。這些結果支持將自動算法設計視為基於驗證的技能調度，而不是一次性代碼生成。
2606.29999.author: Xinyuan Song et.al.
2606.29999.authors: Xinyuan Song, Zekun Cai, Liang Zhao
